current.name: Henry Zimmerman
current.bio: Henry founded AES's first Fed Challenge team, which competed in the Federal Reserve Bank of New York's national research competition on this year's theme: the economics of music. He also designed the Fall term curriculum and helped launch the research colloquium, which puts students in front of Boston policymakers to defend their policy briefs. Outside AES, he does astrophysics research, captains the cross country team, and tutors at the Writing Center.

current.name: Kachi Ikekpeazu
current.bio: Kachi supports student groups in presenting research for the Economics Research Colloquium and leads the overall direction of the program, engaging with policy ideas and communicating them to broader audiences. Outside of economics, Kachi is actively involved in campus leadership as President of Aflatam and Flagstaff Cluster Co-President. He is also a dedicated problem-solver who enjoys tackling Rubik's cubes, and a creative writer who performs slam poetry.

current.name: Andrew Liu
current.bio: Andrew founded and led the publication of the Andover Economics Review. He also founded the AES Policy Think Tank collaboration between students and Boston policy makers, and helps lead the direction of the club's research colloquium. Outside of economics, Andrew loves philosophy and applied ethics, and is a rower on the crew team!
